# INSPIRE-DoJSON

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/inspirehep/inspire-dojson.svg?branch=master
:target: https://travis-ci.org/inspirehep/inspire-dojson
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/inspirehep/inspire-dojson/badge.svg?branch=master
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/inspirehep/inspire-dojson?branch=master
```

## About

INSPIRE-specific rules to transform from MARCXML to JSON and back.

modules.name: inspire_dojson.model
documentation:
Filter-aware subclass of DoJSON's Overdo.

Allows for a list of filters to be passed during instantiation,
which are applied in succession to the result of the DoJSON rules.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.cds.rules.secondary_report_numbers
documentation:
Populate the `037` MARC field.

Also populates the `500`, `595` and `980` MARC field through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.cds.rules.nonfirst_authors
documentation:
Populate `700` MARC field.

Also populates the `701` MARC field through side-effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.cds.rules.urls
documentation:
Populate the `8564` MARC field.

Also populate the `FFT` field through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.isbns2marc
documentation: Populate the `020` MARC field.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.dois
documentation:
Populate the `dois` key.

Also populates the `persistent_identifiers` key through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.dois2marc
documentation: Populate the `0247` MARC field.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.persistent_identifiers2marc
documentation: Populate the `0247` MARC field.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.texkeys
documentation:
Populate the `texkeys` key.

Also populates the `external_system_identifiers` and
`_desy_bookkeeping` keys through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.texkeys2marc
documentation: Populate the `035` MARC field.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.external_system_identifiers2marc
documentation:
Populate the `035` MARC field.

Also populates the `970` MARC field through side effects and an extra
`id_dict` dictionary that holds potentially duplicate IDs that are
post-processed in a filter.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.arxiv_eprints
documentation:
Populate the `arxiv_eprints` key.

Also populates the `report_numbers` key through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.arxiv_eprints2marc
documentation:
Populate the `037` MARC field.

Also populates the `035` and the `65017` MARC fields through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.report_numbers2marc
documentation: Populate the `037` MARC field.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.languages
documentation: Populate the `languages` key.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hep.rules.bd0xx.languages2marc
documentation: Populate the `041` MARC field.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.api.marcxml2record
documentation:
Convert a MARCXML string to a JSON record.

Tries to guess which set of rules to use by inspecting the contents
of the `980__a` MARC field, but falls back to HEP in case nothing
matches, because records belonging to special collections logically
belong to the Literature collection but don't have `980__a:HEP`.

Args:

: marcxml(str): a string containing MARCXML.

Returns:

: dict: a JSON record converted from the string.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.api.record2marcxml_etree
documentation: Convert a JSON record to a MARCXML element tree.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.api.record2marcxml
documentation:
Convert a JSON record to a MARCXML string.

Deduces which set of rules to use by parsing the `$schema` key, as
it unequivocally determines which kind of record we have.

Args:

: record(dict): a JSON record.

Returns:

: str: a MARCXML string converted from the record.

through side effects.

Try to match country name with its code.

Name of the city helps when country_name is "Korea".

documentation: Try to match a string with one of the states in the US.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.geo.parse_conference_address
documentation:
Parse a conference address.

This is a pretty dummy address parser. It only extracts country
and state (for US) and should be replaced with something better,
like Google Geocoding.

documentation: Parse an institution address.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.normalize_isbn
documentation: Normalize an ISBN in order to be schema-compliant.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.normalize_rank
documentation: Normalize a rank in order to be schema-compliant.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.force_single_element
documentation: Force an object to a list and return the first element.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.get_recid_from_ref
documentation:
Retrieve recid from jsonref reference object.

If no recid can be parsed, returns None.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.absolute_url
documentation:
Returns an absolute URL from a URL relative to the server root.

The base URL is taken from the Flask app config if present, otherwise it
falls back to `http://inspirehep.net`.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.afs_url
documentation:
Convert a file path to a URL pointing to its path on AFS.

If `file_path` doesn't start with `/opt/cds-invenio/`, and hence is not on
AFS, it returns it unchanged.

The base AFS path is taken from the Flask app config if present, otherwise
it falls back to `/afs/cern.ch/project/inspire/PROD`.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.afs_url_to_path
documentation:
Convert a AFS URL to its corresponding path on AFS.

If `url` doesn't start with the AFS HTTP service and hence is not on
AFS, it returns it unchanged.

The base AFS path is taken from the Flask app config if present, otherwise
it falls back to `/afs/cern.ch/project/inspire/PROD`.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.get_record_ref
inspire_dojson.utils.strip_empty_values
documentation: Recursively strips empty values.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.dedupe_all_lists
documentation:
Recursively remove duplucates from all lists.

Args:
    obj: collection to deduplicate
    exclude_keys (Container[str]): key names to ignore for deduplication

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.normalize_date_aggressively
documentation: Normalize date, stripping date parts until a valid date is obtained.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.utils.create_record_from_dict
documentation: Create an input record for dojson from a dict.

Populate the `035` MARC field.

Also populates the `8564` and `970` MARC field through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hepnames.rules.name
documentation:
Populate the `name` key.

Also populates the `status`, `birth_date` and `death_date`
keys through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hepnames.rules.name2marc
documentation:
Populates the `100` field.

Also populates the `400`, `880`, and `667` fields through side
effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hepnames.rules.positions
documentation:
Populate the positions field.

Also populates the email_addresses field by side effect.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hepnames.rules.email_addresses2marc
documentation:
Populate the 595 MARCXML field.

Also populates the 371 field as a side effect.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hepnames.rules.email_addresses595
documentation:
Populates the `email_addresses` field using the 595 MARCXML field.

Also populates `_private_notes` as a side effect.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hepnames.rules.arxiv_categories
documentation:
Populate the `arxiv_categories` key.

Also populates the `inspire_categories` key through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hepnames.rules.birth_and_death_date2marc
documentation:
Populate the `100__d` MARC field, which includes the birth and the death date.

By not using the decorator `` `for_each_value` ``, the values of the fields
`` `birth_date` `` and `` `death_date` `` are both added to `` `values` `` as a list.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hepnames.rules.urls
documentation:
Populate the `url` key.

Also populates the `ids` key through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hepnames.rules.new_record
documentation:
Populate the `new_record` key.

Also populates the `ids` key through side effects.

functions.name: inspire_dojson.hepnames.rules.deleted
documentation:
Populate the `deleted` key.

Also populates the `stub` key through side effects.
